content.text: Answer four problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

body.text: State and prove Cayley’s Theorem about finite groups.
Let \(G\) be a group of order \(p^n\), where \(p\) is prime and \(n \geq 1\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Using (a), or otherwise, prove that if \(H\) is a proper subgroup of \(G\), then \(H\) is proper in \(N_G(H)\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Let \(G\) be a group. Prove that \(G\) cannot be expressed as the union of two proper subgroups. (Do not assume that \(G\) is finite.)

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example of a group \(G\) such that \(G\) is the union of three of its proper subgroups.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Determine all groups \(G\) such that \(G\) is the union of all its proper subgroups.

body.text: Prove that there is no simple group of order \(9477 = 3^6 \cdot 13\). (Hint: Consider the action of \(G\) by conjugation on the set \(\operatorname{Syl}_3(G)\) of Sylow \(3\)-subgroups of \(G\).)
Classify the groups of order \(57 = 19 \cdot 3\) up to isomorphism.
